message.content:
## Web Searcher: broad/competitor landscape

Research question: "Competitive landscape and market analysis for AI-powered recruitment platforms targeting solo/independent recruiters and small recruiting agencies (teams of 5-10), with a freemium model (free individual tier + paid team tier).

Research these dimensions specifically:
1. Existing recruitment platforms (ATS, recruiting CRM, applicant tracking) that target solo recruiters and small agencies — e.g. Recruit CRM, Loxo, Crelate, Manatal, JobAdder, Zoho Recruit, Bullhorn, RecruiterFlow, Vincere, iSmartRecruit, Ceipal, Tracker, Hireflow, hiring tools like Workable, Ashby, Greenhouse (note which are NOT for small/solo). For each relevant one: target customer, pricing/tiers, free tier availability, AI features, key strengths/weaknesses.
2. Which of these use a freemium or free-tier model (Zoho Recruit free plan, etc.) and how they monetize. How "easy to start" / self-serve onboarding is positioned.
3. Where AI is being applied in recruiting today (resume parsing, candidate matching/ranking, sourcing, outreach email generation, interview scheduling, candidate Q&A, job description writing, chrome extensions for sourcing from LinkedIn). Which features are table-stakes vs differentiators in 2025-2026.
4. The pain points of small/independent recruiters: data scattered across WhatsApp/Excel/Google Drive, time spent on admin, lack of context on past placements, no revenue visibility. What tools claim to solve these and gaps that remain.
5. Pricing benchmarks: typical per-seat/month pricing for recruiting CRM/ATS at the small-agency level, and what free tiers include.
6. Market size and trends for recruitment software / recruiting CRM, especially the SMB / independent recruiter segment."

Your angle: **broad/competitor landscape** — Surfaces head-to-head comparisons and target-customer fit for the named platforms, establishing who serves solo/small vs enterprise (Bullhorn, Greenhouse, Ashby).
Search query: `best recruiting CRM ATS for solo recruiters and small agencies 2025 comparison Recruit CRM Loxo Crelate Manatal RecruiterFlow Zoho Recruit pricing features`

## Task
Use WebSearch with the query above (or a refined version). Return the top 4-6 most relevant results.
Rank by relevance to the ORIGINAL question, not just the search query. Skip obvious SEO spam/content farms.
Include a short snippet capturing why each result is relevant.

Structured output only.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Links: [{"title":"Zoho Recruit Pricing 2026: Is the Free Tier Worth It? - Pin","url":"https://www.pin.com/blog/zoho-recruit-pricing/"},{"title":"Recruit CRM 2026 Pricing, Features, Reviews & Alternatives | GetApp","url":"https://www.getapp.com/hr-employee-management-software/a/recruit-crm/"},{"title":"Recruiting Software Pricing Guide 2026 - People Managing People","url":"https://peoplemanagingpeople.com/recruitment/recruiting-software-pricing/"},{"title":"Recruitment CRM alternatives, pricing, reviews & best options in 2026","url":"https://www.whitecarrot.ai/resources/blog/recruitment-crm-alternatives"},{"title":"7 Best Free AI Recruiting Tools That Actually Work 2026","url":"https://www.navero.me/blog/free-ai-recruiting-tools-that-actually-work"},{"title":"Top 10 ATS/CRM for Recruiting Agencies (2026) | Spott","url":"https://spott.io/resources/top-10-ats-and-crm-platforms-for-recruiting-agencies-in-2026"},{"title":"AI recruiting software pricing 2026: costs, models compared","url":"https://www.hiretruffle.com/blog/ai-recruiting-software-pricing-guide"}]

I found comprehensive information about recruiting CRM options with freemium models and AI features for solo recruiters and small agencies in 2026. Here are the key findings:

## Freemium Recruiting CRM Options

**Zoho Recruit:**
Starts at $0/month on the Forever Free plan and scales to $75/user/month on the Staffing Agency Enterprise tier, billed annually. However, with just one active job slot and no resume parsing, AI features, or sourcing tools, it's functional only for a solo recruiter filling a single position at a time. AI shows up at Professional ($50/user/mo), with Zia candidate matching, AI assessments, SMS, and phone integration gated behind the Pro tier.

**Whitecarrot.ai:**
Whitecarrot's free plan includes AI-powered screening, pipeline management, and job posting, making it one of the most accessible options for lean hiring teams in 2026.

**Loxo:**
Loxo combines ATS, CRM, and an extensive sourcing database in a single platform, with the free tier making it accessible for solo recruiters and small shops.

## Pricing Models

Manatal at $15/user/month and Loxo's free tier give you functional ATS/CRM capabilities at a fraction of the cost. Per-user pricing doesn't scale, and at 5+ users, flat-rate platforms beat Zoho's per-seat model.

## Key Considerations for Solo Recruiters

Solo recruiters and teams under 20 roles operate effectively within free limitations.

Links: [{"title":"15 Best CRMs for Staffing Companies to Win Talent in 2026","url":"https://www.cloudtalk.io/blog/best-crm-for-staffing-companies/"},{"title":"18 Best Recruitment CRM Software for Agencies in 2026 -","url":"https://recruiterflow.com/blog/best-recruitment-crm-software/"},{"title":"13 Best Recruitment CRM Systems in 2026 (Tested) | Leonar","url":"https://www.leonar.app/blog/best-recruitment-crm/"},{"title":"Top 7 RecruitCRM alternatives for recruitment agencies in 2026 | Happlicant: The Best ATS/CRM for Recruiters","url":"https://www.happlicant.com/blog/top-7-recruitcrm-alternatives-for-recruitment-agencies-in-2026"},{"title":"Best Recruitment CRM for Agencies in 2026: An Honest Comparison","url":"https://automindz-solutions.com/blog/best-recruitment-crm-for-agencies-2026"},{"title":"11 Best ATS CRM for Recruiting Agencies | Leonar","url":"https://www.leonar.app/blog/best-ats-crm-software-recruiting-agencies/"},{"title":"Compare Manatal and Recruit CRM & ATS","url":"https://www.g2.com/compare/manatal-vs-recruit-crm-ats"},{"title":"Introducing G2.ai, the future of software buying.Try now","url":"https://g2.com/compare/recruit-crm-ats-vs-zoho-recruit"}]

Based on the search results, here's a comprehensive comparison of top recruiting CRM/ATS platforms for solo recruiters and small agencies in 2025-2026:

## **Best Platforms by Use Case**

**For Solo Recruiters & Tight Budgets:**
- **Recruit CRM** at $85/user/month - Clean, fast, and affordable with productive setup in hours
- **Manatal** at $15/user/month - surprisingly capable for the price if bootstrapping on a tight budget

**For Small/Mid-Sized Agencies (5-20 people):**
- **Recruiterflow, Crelate, or Recruit CRM** - Affordable, focused, and fast to adopt
- **RecruiterFlow** or **Recruit CRM** at this stage when automation matters more than feature depth, with RecruiterFlow's built-in email sequences and workflow automation saving you from buying separate tools

## **Key Features & Differentiators**

**Loxo:**
- AI-powered CRM combining ATS, CRM, and sourcing automation designed for agencies wanting advanced data tools and multi-channel outreach
- Estimated from $500/month depending on team size and features

**Crelate:**
- Integrates with job boards, email systems, productivity tools, and background check providers
- Starts from $99/user/month with higher tiers offering advanced reporting and customization

**Manatal:**
- Modern recruitment CRM with strong AI capabilities, resume parsing, and automated candidate matching, known for affordability and ease of use, ideal for small to mid-sized agencies

**Zoho Recruit:**
- Both an ATS and CRM for HR teams, offering candidate pipelines, automation, and job board posting
- Free tier available for solo recruiters or very small teams already invested in the Zoho ecosystem

**RecruiterFlow:**
- Particularly strong if email automation is central to sourcing and engaging at around $119/user/month

## **Pricing Overview**
Free or under $50/month: Zoho Recruit (free plan), Manatal ($15/user/mo), Crelate ($69), Recruit CRM ($85), Recruiterflow ($99)

input.query: Bullhorn Greenhouse Ashby enterprise vs small recruiter independent recruiter pain points data scattered WhatsApp Excel admin time

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Bullhorn Greenhouse Ashby enterprise vs small recruiter independent recruiter pain points data scattered WhatsApp Excel admin time"

Links: [{"title":"Best Recruitment Analytics Software in 2026 (12 Tools Reviewed)","url":"https://100hires.com/best-recruitment-analytics-software.html"},{"title":"All-in-one Recruiting Software for the Enterprise | Ashby","url":"https://www.ashbyhq.com/enterprise"},{"title":"All-in-one Recruiting Software for Ambitious Teams | Ashby","url":"https://www.ashbyhq.com/"},{"title":"Bullhorn vs Greenhouse | Which Recruitment Software Wins In 2026?","url":"https://www.selecthub.com/recruiting-software/bullhorn-vs-greenhouse-recruiting/"},{"title":"Ashby vs Greenhouse: Honest Comparison for 2026 (With Real User Data)","url":"https://100hires.com/ashby-vs-greenhouse.html"},{"title":"Top recruitment agency software: the best tools for staffing firms in 2026 | Metaview Blog","url":"https://www.metaview.ai/resources/blog/recruitment-agency-software"},{"title":"13 Best ATS for Startups: Ashby, Greenhouse, Lever, & More - Arc Employer Blog","url":"https://arc.dev/employer-blog/13-best-ats-for-startups-ashby-greenhouse-lever/"},{"title":"15 Best ATS Systems for Recruiters in 2026 (Tested) | Leonar","url":"https://www.leonar.app/blog/best-ats-recruitment/"},{"title":"Compare Ashby vs Bullhorn","url":"https://www.cbinsights.com/compare/ashby-vs-bullhorn"},{"title":"Compare Bullhorn vs Greenhouse","url":"https://www.cbinsights.com/compare/bullhorn-vs-greenhouse"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about these recruiting platforms and their pain points:

## Platform Positioning

Ashby is positioned for analytics-first mid-market, while Greenhouse is recommended for structured enterprise hiring. Bullhorn is an ATS platform designed for staffing and recruitment agencies, offering features to help staffing agencies manage job orders, candidate sourcing, and placements.

## Pain Points for Different Recruiter Types

### Admin Time & Efficiency
Agency software is built around multiple clients and many concurrent mandates per recruiter, while general ATSs like Greenhouse or Ashby map to job reqs and stages, so agencies running them end up bolting on custom fields and workarounds to make the basic agency workflow work. Ashby addresses admin time through automation - you can set up different things to be automated between each step of the process, saving countless hours of admin time.

### Data & Consolidation
Ashby's all-in-one platform bundles ATS, CRM, sourcing sequences, interview scheduling, and analytics into one product, with multiple Reddit users reporting dropping Calendly, DocuSign, and separate sourcing tools after switching. This addresses the pain point of scattered data across tools.

### Enterprise vs. Small Recruiter Differences
Most Ashby customers are in the 50-1,000 employee range, while large organizations (2,000+) still lean toward Greenhouse's track record.

The search results don't contain specific information about WhatsApp or Excel as pain points for independent recruiters, but the general pattern shows that modern platforms like Ashby are addressing the fragmentation problem for smaller teams.

content.text: I have enough to rank the most relevant results across all dimensions of the question.

results.title: Zoho Recruit Pricing 2026: Is the Free Tier Worth It?

Details Zoho Recruit's Forever Free plan ($0) limited to one active job slot with no resume parsing, AI, or sourcing, scaling to $50/user/mo (Professional, where Zia AI matching/assessments unlock) and $75/user/mo (Staffing Agency Enterprise, annual). Notes per-seat models stop being competitive at 5+ users vs flat-rate platforms, which is core to the team-tier monetization question.

results.title: 18 Best Recruitment CRM Software for Agencies in 2026

Useful for target-customer fit and pricing/AI feature comparison at the small-agency level, with RecruiterFlow positioned around built-in email sequences and workflow automation.

results.title: Top 10 ATS/CRM for Recruiting Agencies (2026)
results.snippet: Covers Loxo's free tier making it accessible for solo recruiters and small shops, Manatal at $15/user/mo, and the trade-off between per-user vs flat-rate pricing. Strong on the 'who serves solo/small' question and where free tiers fit, plus AI-powered screening/sourcing as table-stakes.

Directly supports the 'who serves solo/small agency vs enterprise' positioning and the data-consolidation pain point.

All-in-one bundle (ATS, CRM, sourcing sequences, scheduling, analytics) shows the consolidation trend that replaces Calendly/DocuSign/standalone sourcing tools, useful as a contrast to the solo/small target.

results.snippet: Benchmarks AI recruiting software pricing models, helping establish where AI features sit relative to per-seat cost and which AI capabilities (matching, screening, sourcing, outreach) are differentiators vs table-stakes in 2026.
